arxiv_papers.title: Assessing foundational atomistic models for iron alloys under Earth's core conditions
arxiv_papers.snippet: We assess the capability of recently developed foundational atomistic models (FAMs) to simulate iron alloys under the extreme pressures and temperatures of Earth's core. Static equations of state of hexagonal close-packed (hcp) and body-centered cubic (bcc) iron computed by 17 FAMs are benchmarked a

arxiv_papers.title: Metal Saturation and the Redistribution of Hydrogen in Earth's Mantle
arxiv_papers.snippet: Iron disproportionation reactions in mantle silicates can produce metallic iron that drives Earth's deep mantle toward metal saturation under reduced conditions. podcast: [The wildlife sanctuary you can visit from anywhere | Maya Higa] Creator Maya Higa is on a mission to use the internet to build the next generation of conservationists. Her virtual education center, Alveus Sanctuary, is one of the most-watched sanctuaries on Earth, with dozens of rescued animals and cameras livestreaming to a community of millions inspired to help protect the wildlife. youtube_items.title: [YT] FILE 007: Climate Engineering — Hubris or Necessity?
youtube_items.summary: File number 0007. Topic: Climate engineering. Hubris or necessity. Parties. The engineer versus the ecologist. The planet [music] is warming. 1.5° C above pre-industrial levels. Glacias melting. Coral reefs bleaching. [music] Extreme weather intensifying. We set targets. We missed them. We made promises. We broke them. Carbon reduction isn't happening fast enough. The math is brutal. We're out of time. So, some scientists propose a different solution. If we can't stop warming the planet, [music] what if we call it manually? Climate engineering, geoengineering, planetary scale intervention. youtube_items.title: [YT] Does cleaner air make the planet hotter?
youtube_items.summary: [music] This is Climate Watch. [music] I'm Jaoyang. Welcome to the show. [music] China has seen a massive rapid cleanup of its skies over the past decade. By slashing coal use and upgrading power plants, the country has cleared the smog that once defined many of its cities, significantly improving public health. But lately, a startling counternarrative has emerged in the headlines. An article in The Economist argued that China's air quality improvements have hastened global warming. Meanwhile, research cited by Australia's ABC News suggests the cleanup may even be driving hotter and drier conditions in Australia. But how could cleaning up pollution possibly make the planet warmer? Well, this has everything to do with aerosols, the tiny particles in the atmosphere that play a complex role i
